title: Scenario pack frontmatter
description: Validates the optional YAML frontmatter of a Specify scenario file. The discoverer in `specdev lint` only validates files that live under an accepted scenario root and start with frontmatter. Bodies of opted-in scenarios follow the standard sections (`Intent`, `Workspace`, `Inputs`, `Invocation`, `Expected Artifacts`, `Assertions`, `Negative Expectations`, `Cleanup`).

id.description: Globally unique scenario identifier (kebab-case). Cross-file uniqueness is enforced in `specdev lint`.

owner.description: The adapter, plugin, skill, or suite that owns this scenario (kebab-case).

kind.description: Open enum. C03 actively validates `adapter` and `adapter-boundary`; `suite` and `skill` are accepted so C06/skill-fixture promotions can opt in without re-touching this schema.

adapter.description: Versioned adapter identifier, e.g. `contracts@v1`. Required when `kind` is `adapter` or `adapter-boundary` (enforced by allOf below).

backend.description: Scenario execution style. `manual` documents a human-driven scenario; `agent`, `recorded`, and `fixture` are reserved for future automation.

stages.description: Slice phases the scenario exercises. Must be a contiguous slice of `[plan, refine, build, merge, drop]` anchored at any element; the prefix rule is enforced in `specdev lint` because JSON Schema cannot express ordering without combinatorial enums.

isolation.description: Workspace isolation contract for the scenario.

assertions.description: Optional list of named assertion ids. Free-form kebab-case strings.

expected-artifacts.description: Optional list of relative artifact paths produced by the scenario. Path-safety (relative, no `..`, no absolute) is enforced in `specdev lint`.

negative-expectations.description: Forbidden-condition ids. Required (with at least one entry) when `kind` is `adapter-boundary`.
